differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) with Lupus Nephritis and secondary antiphospholipid syndrome
Membranous Nephropathy with secondary renal vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism
next_steps: Perform comprehensive autoimmune workup: ANA, dsDNA, complement (C3/C4), and antiphospholipid antibody panel
Urgent inpatient nephrology consultation for consideration of renal biopsy
Initiate therapeutic anticoagulation (e.g., heparin infusion) for confirmed renal vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism
Transthoracic or transesophageal echocardiography to evaluate for valvular vegetations or intracardiac thrombus
Continue broad-spectrum antibiotics pending blood and sputum culture results
Avoid prophylactic fluid restriction until hemodynamic stability is achieved and volume status is fully assessed
Consult rheumatology for potential escalation to immunosuppressive therapy if autoimmune disease is confirmed
